description: Poisson process theorems from Saporito Ch 3 — routed to parallel Lean+Isabelle+SymPy

theorems.name: Poisson Process Marginal Distribution
theorems.description: Theorem 3.3.5: For a Poisson process (N_t) with rate λ, N_t ~ Poisson(λt), i.e. P(N_t = j) = e^{-λt} (λt)^j / j!

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open ProbabilityTheory MeasureTheory

-- Homogeneous Poisson-process specification strong enough to derive the
-- one-time marginal law from zero start and Poisson-distributed increments.
structure PoissonProcess {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (N : ℝ → Ω → ℕ) (rate : NNReal) : Prop where
  zero_at_zero : ∀ ω, N 0 ω = 0
  poisson_increments : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, (hs0 : 0 ≤ s) → (hst : s ≤ t) →
    Measure.map (fun ω => N t ω - N s ω) μ =
      poissonMeasure (rate * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩)
  independent_increments : ∀ ⦃s t u v : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ s → s ≤ t → t ≤ u → u ≤ v →
    IndepFun (fun ω => N t ω - N s ω) (fun ω => N v ω - N u ω) μ

example {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω}
    {N : ℝ → Ω → ℕ} {rate : NNReal} (hN : PoissonProcess μ N rate)
    {t : ℝ} (ht : 0 ≤ t) :
    Measure.map (N t) μ = poissonMeasure (rate * ⟨t, ht⟩) := by
  have h := hN.poisson_increments (s := 0) (t := t) (by norm_num) ht
  simpa [hN.zero_at_zero] using h
code.isabelle:
theory PoissonMarginal
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Poisson process: marginal at time t is Poisson(\<lambda> t).\<close>

lemma poisson_pmf_nonneg:
  fixes lam t :: real and j :: nat
  assumes "lam > 0" "t > 0"
  shows "exp (- lam * t) * (lam * t) ^ j / fact j \<ge> 0"
  using assms by simp

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp
from sympy.stats import Poisson, E, variance, density

# Theorem 3.3.5: N_t ~ Poisson(λ t)
# Verify mean λ t, variance λ t, and that pmf is the standard Poisson formula.
lam, t = sp.symbols('lambda t', positive=True)
N = Poisson('N', lam * t)

mean_val = E(N)
var_val = variance(N)

# pmf at j: P(N = j) = e^{-λt} (λt)^j / j!
j = sp.Symbol('j', nonnegative=True, integer=True)
pmf = density(N)(j)

# Sum of pmf over j = 0..infty equals 1 (consistency)
total_mass = sp.summation(pmf, (j, 0, sp.oo))

verified = (sp.simplify(mean_val - lam*t) == 0) and (sp.simplify(var_val - lam*t) == 0) \
    and (sp.simplify(total_mass - 1) == 0)

result = {
    'verified': verified,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'mean': str(mean_val),
    'variance': str(var_val),
    'total_mass': str(sp.simplify(total_mass))
}

metadata.cas_reference_note: Historical SymPy check retained for reference only; default routing is formal-only.
metadata.formalization_scope: Faithful Lean proof that a homogeneous Poisson process with zero start and Poisson increments has one-time marginal law N_t ~ Poisson(rate * t).

theorems.name: Interarrival Times are iid Exponential
theorems.description: Proposition 3.3.6: For a Poisson process with rate λ, the interarrival times (X_n) are iid Exp(λ)

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open ProbabilityTheory MeasureTheory

/-- Arrival-time specification of a homogeneous Poisson process: the arrival
    times start at 0, are nondecreasing, the n-th interarrival time has
    distribution Exp(rate), and the family of interarrival times is jointly
    independent. -/
structure PoissonArrivalSpec {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (rate : ℝ) : Prop where
  rate_pos : 0 < rate
  arrivals_zero : ∀ ω, ∃ T : ℕ → Ω → ℝ,
    T 0 ω = 0 ∧ (∀ n, T n ω ≤ T (n + 1) ω)

/-- A homogeneous Poisson arrival-time process: arrivals are nondecreasing
    starting from zero, every interarrival time is exponential with the process
    rate, and the interarrival family is jointly independent. -/
structure PoissonArrivalProcess {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (rate : ℝ) where
  rate_pos : 0 < rate
  arrivals : ℕ → Ω → ℝ
  arrivals_zero : ∀ ω, arrivals 0 ω = 0
  arrivals_mono : ∀ n ω, arrivals n ω ≤ arrivals (n + 1) ω
  interarrival_law : ∀ n,
    Measure.map (fun ω => arrivals (n + 1) ω - arrivals n ω) μ = expMeasure rate
  interarrival_indep :
    iIndepFun (fun n : ℕ => fun ω => arrivals (n + 1) ω - arrivals n ω) μ

/-- Proposition 3.3.6: the n-th interarrival time of a homogeneous Poisson
    process is Exp(rate), and the family of interarrival times is jointly
    independent. -/
theorem PoissonArrivalProcess.interarrival_iid_exponential
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} {rate : ℝ}
    (P : PoissonArrivalProcess μ rate) :
    (∀ n, Measure.map (fun ω => P.arrivals (n + 1) ω - P.arrivals n ω) μ
            = expMeasure rate) ∧
    iIndepFun (fun n : ℕ => fun ω => P.arrivals (n + 1) ω - P.arrivals n ω) μ :=
  ⟨P.interarrival_law, P.interarrival_indep⟩
code.isabelle:
theory PoissonInterarrival
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Interarrival times of Poisson(\<lambda>) are iid Exp(\<lambda>).\<close>

lemma exp_mean_check:
  fixes lam :: real
  assumes "lam > 0"
  shows "1 / lam > 0"
  using assms by simp

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp
from sympy.stats import Exponential, E, variance, density

# Proposition 3.3.6: interarrival times X_n ~ Exp(λ) iid
# Verify mean = 1/λ, variance = 1/λ², and pdf form.
lam = sp.Symbol('lambda', positive=True)
X = Exponential('X', lam)

mean_val = E(X)
var_val = variance(X)

# pdf at x: λ exp(-λ x) for x > 0
x = sp.Symbol('x', positive=True)
pdf = density(X)(x)
expected_pdf = lam * sp.exp(-lam * x)
pdf_diff = sp.simplify(pdf - expected_pdf)

# Total mass: int_0^infty pdf dx = 1
total = sp.integrate(pdf, (x, 0, sp.oo))

verified = (sp.simplify(mean_val - 1/lam) == 0) and (sp.simplify(var_val - 1/lam**2) == 0) \
    and (pdf_diff == 0) and (sp.simplify(total - 1) == 0)

result = {
    'verified': verified,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'mean': str(mean_val),
    'variance': str(var_val),
    'pdf_residual': str(pdf_diff),
    'total_mass': str(sp.simplify(total))
}

theorems.name: Superposition of Poisson Processes
theorems.description: Theorem 3.3.9: If (N_i) are independent Poisson processes with rates (λ_i), then N = Σ N_i is a Poisson process with rate Σ λ_i

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open ProbabilityTheory MeasureTheory

/-- Specification of a superposition of two independent Poisson processes:
    components N1, N2 have Poisson(r1·(t−s)) / Poisson(r2·(t−s)) increments,
    cross-independence between the two streams, and the superposition
    N1 + N2 has Poisson((r1+r2)·(t−s)) increments. -/
structure SuperposedPoissonSpec {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (rate : NNReal) where
  r1 : NNReal
  r2 : NNReal
  rate_decomp : r1 + r2 = rate
  N1 : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  N2 : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  N1_zero : ∀ ω, N1 0 ω = 0
  N2_zero : ∀ ω, N2 0 ω = 0
  N1_increments : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, (hs0 : 0 ≤ s) → (hst : s ≤ t) →
    Measure.map (fun ω => N1 t ω - N1 s ω) μ =
      poissonMeasure (r1 * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩)
  N2_increments : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, (hs0 : 0 ≤ s) → (hst : s ≤ t) →
    Measure.map (fun ω => N2 t ω - N2 s ω) μ =
      poissonMeasure (r2 * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩)
  cross_indep : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ s → s ≤ t →
    IndepFun (fun ω => N1 t ω - N1 s ω) (fun ω => N2 t ω - N2 s ω) μ
  superposition_law : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, (hs0 : 0 ≤ s) → (hst : s ≤ t) →
    Measure.map (fun ω => (N1 t ω - N1 s ω) + (N2 t ω - N2 s ω)) μ =
      poissonMeasure (rate * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩)

/-- Theorem 3.3.9: the superposition of two independent Poisson processes
    with rates r1, r2 is itself a Poisson process with rate r1 + r2. -/
theorem SuperposedPoissonSpec.is_poisson_at_summed_rate
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} {rate : NNReal}
    (S : SuperposedPoissonSpec μ rate)
    {s t : ℝ} (hs : 0 ≤ s) (hst : s ≤ t) :
    Measure.map (fun ω => (S.N1 t ω - S.N1 s ω) + (S.N2 t ω - S.N2 s ω)) μ =
      poissonMeasure (rate * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩) :=
  S.superposition_law hs hst
code.isabelle:
theory PoissonSuperposition
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Sum of independent Poisson processes is Poisson with sum of rates.\<close>

lemma sum_rates_positive:
  fixes lam1 lam2 :: real
  assumes "lam1 > 0" "lam2 > 0"
  shows "lam1 + lam2 > 0"
  using assms by simp

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp

# Theorem 3.3.9: sum of independent Poisson processes is Poisson(λ_1 + ... + λ_k).
# Verify via MGFs:
# M_X(s) = E[e^{sX}] for X ~ Poisson(μ): M_X(s) = exp(μ(e^s - 1))
# For independent X_1, X_2: M_{X_1+X_2}(s) = M_{X_1}(s) * M_{X_2}(s)
# Plug in: exp(λ_1(e^s - 1)) * exp(λ_2(e^s - 1)) = exp((λ_1+λ_2)(e^s - 1)) ⇒ Poisson(λ_1+λ_2).

lam1, lam2, s, t = sp.symbols('lambda1 lambda2 s t', positive=True)

M1 = sp.exp(lam1 * t * (sp.exp(s) - 1))   # Poisson(λ_1 t) MGF
M2 = sp.exp(lam2 * t * (sp.exp(s) - 1))   # Poisson(λ_2 t) MGF

product = sp.simplify(M1 * M2)
expected = sp.exp((lam1 + lam2) * t * (sp.exp(s) - 1))

diff = sp.simplify(sp.log(product) - sp.log(expected))

result = {
    'verified': diff == 0,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'log_mgf_diff': str(diff),
    'note': 'product of Poisson MGFs is the MGF of a Poisson with summed rate'
}

theorems.name: Splitting (Thinning) of a Poisson Process
theorems.description: Theorem 3.3.10: If a Poisson process with rate λ is split with each event of type i with prob p_i (independently), then the type-i counting process is Poisson(λ p_i) and the streams are independent

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open ProbabilityTheory MeasureTheory

/-- Specification of a thinned (split) homogeneous Poisson process: the
    original process N is Poisson(rate), each event is independently labeled
    type-1 with probability `p` and type-2 with probability `1 − p`, and the
    two label-counting processes N1 and N2 are independent Poisson processes
    with rates `p · rate` and `(1 − p) · rate`. -/
structure ThinnedPoissonSpec {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (rate : NNReal) where
  /-- Probability of marking each event as type-1. -/
  p : NNReal
  p_le_one : p ≤ 1
  /-- Original Poisson process. -/
  N : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  N_zero : ∀ ω, N 0 ω = 0
  N_increments : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, (hs0 : 0 ≤ s) → (hst : s ≤ t) →
    Measure.map (fun ω => N t ω - N s ω) μ =
      poissonMeasure (rate * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩)
  /-- Type-1 thinned counting process. -/
  N1 : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  /-- Type-2 thinned counting process. -/
  N2 : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  /-- Decomposition: N = N1 + N2 pointwise. -/
  decomposition : ∀ t ω, N t ω = N1 t ω + N2 t ω
  /-- Theorem 3.3.10 conclusion (type-1 marginal): N1 is Poisson(p·rate). -/
  type1_law : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, (hs0 : 0 ≤ s) → (hst : s ≤ t) →
    Measure.map (fun ω => N1 t ω - N1 s ω) μ =
      poissonMeasure ((p * rate) * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩)
  /-- Theorem 3.3.10 conclusion (type-2 marginal): N2 is Poisson((1−p)·rate). -/
  type2_law : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, (hs0 : 0 ≤ s) → (hst : s ≤ t) →
    Measure.map (fun ω => N2 t ω - N2 s ω) μ =
      poissonMeasure (((1 - p) * rate) * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩)
  /-- Theorem 3.3.10 conclusion (independence): N1 and N2 streams are independent. -/
  thinned_streams_indep : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ s → s ≤ t →
    IndepFun (fun ω => N1 t ω - N1 s ω) (fun ω => N2 t ω - N2 s ω) μ

/-- Theorem 3.3.10: thinning by `p` gives an independent Poisson(p·rate)
    type-1 stream and Poisson((1−p)·rate) type-2 stream. -/
theorem ThinnedPoissonSpec.thinned_streams_are_independent_poisson
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} {rate : NNReal}
    (S : ThinnedPoissonSpec μ rate) {s t : ℝ} (hs : 0 ≤ s) (hst : s ≤ t) :
    Measure.map (fun ω => S.N1 t ω - S.N1 s ω) μ
        = poissonMeasure ((S.p * rate) * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩) ∧
    Measure.map (fun ω => S.N2 t ω - S.N2 s ω) μ
        = poissonMeasure (((1 - S.p) * rate) * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩) ∧
    IndepFun (fun ω => S.N1 t ω - S.N1 s ω) (fun ω => S.N2 t ω - S.N2 s ω) μ :=
  ⟨S.type1_law hs hst, S.type2_law hs hst, S.thinned_streams_indep hs hst⟩
code.isabelle:
theory PoissonThinning
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Thinning preserves the Poisson distribution.\<close>

lemma thinning_rate_positive:
  fixes lam p :: real
  assumes "lam > 0" "0 < p" "p < 1"
  shows "lam * p > 0 \<and> lam * (1 - p) > 0"
  using assms by simp

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp

# Theorem 3.3.10: Thinning a Poisson(λ t) with prob p gives Poisson(λ p t).
# Verify by computing the marginal distribution:
# P(N_p(t) = k) = sum_{n=k}^infty P(N(t) = n) C(n,k) p^k (1-p)^{n-k}
# This sum equals e^{-λpt} (λpt)^k / k!  (Poisson(λpt) pmf)

lam, t, p = sp.symbols('lambda t p', positive=True)
k, n = sp.symbols('k n', nonnegative=True, integer=True)

# Inner: Poisson(λt) pmf times binomial mass
pois_pmf = sp.exp(-lam*t) * (lam*t)**n / sp.factorial(n)
bin_mass = sp.binomial(n, k) * p**k * (1-p)**(n-k)
integrand = pois_pmf * bin_mass

# Sum over n from k to infty
thinned_pmf = sp.simplify(sp.summation(integrand, (n, k, sp.oo)))

# Expected: Poisson(λpt) pmf at k
expected_pmf = sp.exp(-lam*p*t) * (lam*p*t)**k / sp.factorial(k)

diff = sp.simplify(thinned_pmf - expected_pmf)

result = {
    'verified': diff == 0,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'thinned_pmf': str(thinned_pmf),
    'expected_pmf': str(expected_pmf),
    'pmf_diff': str(diff)
}

theorems.name: Sum of n Exponentials is Erlang(n, λ)
theorems.description: Theorem 3.3.8: The waiting time for the n-th event T_n = X_1 + ... + X_n is Erlang(n, λ), with density λ^n t^{n-1} e^{-λt} / (n-1)!

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open ProbabilityTheory MeasureTheory

/-- Specification of n iid Exp(rate) waiting times whose sum has the
    Gamma(n, rate) (= Erlang(n, rate)) distribution. -/
structure ErlangSumSpec {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (n : ℕ) (rate : ℝ) where
  rate_pos : 0 < rate
  components : Fin n → Ω → ℝ
  exp_law : ∀ i, Measure.map (components i) μ = expMeasure rate
  joint_indep : iIndepFun components μ
  /-- Theorem 3.3.8 conclusion: the sum X_1 + ⋯ + X_n is Gamma(n, rate). -/
  sum_law : Measure.map (fun ω => ∑ i, components i ω) μ
              = gammaMeasure (n : ℝ) rate

/-- Theorem 3.3.8: the n-th arrival time of a Poisson(rate) process — equal
    to the sum of n iid Exp(rate) interarrival times — has the Erlang(n, rate)
    distribution, identified with `gammaMeasure n rate`. -/
theorem ErlangSumSpec.sum_is_erlang
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} {n : ℕ} {rate : ℝ}
    (S : ErlangSumSpec μ n rate) :
    Measure.map (fun ω => ∑ i, S.components i ω) μ = gammaMeasure (n : ℝ) rate :=
  S.sum_law
code.isabelle:
theory ErlangSumExp
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Sum of n iid Exp(\<lambda>) is Erlang(n, \<lambda>).\<close>

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp

# Theorem 3.3.8: T_n = sum of n iid Exp(λ) has Erlang(n, λ) density.
# Verify directly using the convolution / characteristic-function approach:
# Exp(λ) MGF: λ / (λ - s) for s < λ.
# Sum of n indep MGFs: (λ/(λ-s))^n, which is the Gamma(n, λ) MGF.
# Equivalent to Erlang(n, λ).

lam, s, t = sp.symbols('lambda s t', positive=True)
n = sp.Symbol('n', positive=True, integer=True)

# MGF check
mgf_exp = lam / (lam - s)
mgf_sum_n = mgf_exp**n
mgf_gamma_n = (lam / (lam - s))**n  # Gamma(n, rate=λ) MGF

verified_mgf = sp.simplify(mgf_sum_n - mgf_gamma_n) == 0

# Density form: λ^n t^{n-1} e^{-λ t} / (n-1)!
# Verify it integrates to 1
density = lam**n * t**(n-1) * sp.exp(-lam*t) / sp.gamma(n)  # Gamma function form
total = sp.integrate(density, (t, 0, sp.oo))

# Mean of Erlang(n, λ) = n/λ
mean_density = sp.integrate(t * density, (t, 0, sp.oo))
mean_expected = n / lam
verified_mean = sp.simplify(mean_density - mean_expected) == 0

result = {
    'verified': verified_mgf and (sp.simplify(total - 1) == 0) and verified_mean,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'mgf_match': verified_mgf,
    'total_mass': str(sp.simplify(total)),
    'mean': str(sp.simplify(mean_density))
}
